Prologue

It started with the sound.

At first, it was subtle—just a low, distant hum in the dark. Then came the growls, rising like a storm just beyond the perimeter. By the time the guards realized what was happening, it was already too late.

The raiders were back.

But this time, they weren’t alone.

The bastards had figured out how to control the hordes, luring them with makeshift noise machines strapped to the backs of their vehicles. The groans of the undead, the rumble of engines, and the mocking laughter of the raiders blended into a nightmarish symphony.

Panic erupted as the school’s alarms blared to life, their piercing wails echoing through the empty halls. Survivors scrambled from their makeshift rooms, clutching what little they could carry as the guards shouted orders.

“Everyone, move! Evacuate now!” one shouted over the chaos.

Background

Isabella

Three months ago, everything fell apart, but for Isabella, it still feels like yesterday. Back then, she was the kind of teacher everyone loved. Her short white hair, always neatly combed, and those piercing blue eyes made her look like some kind of angel walking the halls of Waldrack High.
